articles.title: Rubio rejects proposal to charge ships for using Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Rubio rejects proposal to charge ships for using Strait of Hormuz US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has dismissed the idea of imposing fees on vessels using the Strait of Hormuz, saying Washington would oppose any attempt to charge for passage through the strategic waterway. "The whole world will be against any mechanism that charges money to use an international waterway," Rubio told reporters. "It's that simple. articles.title: Brent crude drops below $75 for first time since war on Iran
articles.summary: Brent crude drops below $75 for first time since war on Iran Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, has fallen below $75 a barrel for the first time since before the US-Israeli war on Iran began. The benchmark was trading at $74.80 per barrel at 12:34 GMT on Tuesday, according to Bloomberg. The drop marks the first time Brent has traded below $75 since 27 February, the day before the outbreak of the war, according to Reuters, as markets continue to respond positively to progress in negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

articles.title: ESPN's Linda Cohn Reflects on Historic SportsCenter Run
articles.summary: ESPN legend and sports broadcasting trailblazer Linda Cohn is stepping away from the anchor desk following a historic career that spanned more than 30 years. Cohn joins Bloomberg Intelligence Radio to reflect on the journey that made her the longest-tenured SportsCenter anchor in the network's history. From anchoring thousands of shows to covering unforgettable moments in sports, Cohn discusses the passion that fueled her career, her favorite memories over the years, and why helping inspire the next generation of women in broadcasting means so much to her. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US embassy in Kuwait resumes operations after wartime suspension
articles.summary: US embassy in Kuwait resumes operations after wartime suspension The US embassy in Kuwait has resumed operations after services were suspended following Iranian attacks earlier this year, a US state department spokesperson has said. The embassy will immediately restart emergency services for US citizens, while other consular services will be restored in phases, the spokesperson said. The state department suspended operations at its embassies in Kuwait and Saudi Arabia after Iranian drones targeted the diplomatic missions following the outbreak of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: Palestinian man killed by Israeli forces near Jenin
articles.summary: Palestinian man killed by Israeli forces near Jenin  A 29-year-old Palestinian man has been killed by Israeli forces in the town of al-Yamoun, west of Jenin, according to the Palestinian Ministry of Health. The ministry identified the victim as Muhammad Nazem Ezzat Zayed and said Israeli forces took his body after shooting him. According to the ministry, Zayed's killing brings the number of Palestinians killed by Israeli forces in the occupied West Bank since the start of the year to 71.

articles.title: Lebanon transfers second batch of Syrian convicts to Damascus
articles.summary: Lebanon transfers second batch of Syrian convicts to Damascus Lebanon transferred 128 Syrian prisoners to their home country on Wednesday, a Lebanese security source told AFP, in the second such handover under an agreement reached earlier this year. The source, who spoke on condition of anonymity, said the prisoners were transferred to Damascus to serve the remainder of their sentences in Syria. The move follows the transfer of more than 130 Syrian detainees in March.
